info.name: Unified Agents SDK
info.description:
Complete Postman collection for the Unified Agents SDK — kept in sync with README.md (MCP two paths, bridges, dynamic markdown contexts, provider profiles).

Two endpoints:
- POST /agent-query         — synchronous, returns JSON
- POST /agent-query/stream  — Server-Sent Events (SSE)

All requests share the same body schema (AgentQueryRequest).
Fields marked OPTIONAL can be omitted.

MCP: see README section **MCP — Model Context Protocol**. Folder **11** matches README scenarios (named github_mcp preset, provider-native options.mcp_servers, Claude Agent). Folders **3–4** = gateway Path 1; folder **11.2** = Path 2.

Bring-your-own-key (BYOK): optional top-level field `provider_credentials` with `api_key`, optional `model`, optional `base_url` (OpenAI-compatible only). Requires `ALLOW_PER_REQUEST_PROVIDER_CREDENTIALS=true` in gateway .env — default is false (403 if you send real credentials while disabled).

Variables — set in the collection or an environment:
  base_url           http://localhost:8000
  openai_key         paste here for BYOK OpenAI examples (same secret you could put in gateway .env)
  anthropic_key      sk-ant-...
  google_key         AI...
  groq_key           gsk-...
  mcp_server_url     http://your-mcp-server/mcp
  rag_url            http://your-rag-service/search
  tool_url           http://your-tool-service/run

item.name: 1 — Basic Queries
item.description: Minimal requests showing the simplest possible usage. All fields except 'input' are OPTIONAL.

request.description: Only 'input' is required. Uses the 'default' profile and global provider from .env.

response.name: 200 OK — success
response.body:
{
  "output": "The capital of France is Paris.",
  "tool_traces": [],
  "usage": { "input_tokens": 14, "output_tokens": 9 },
  "provider": "openai_compatible",
  "model": "gpt-4o",
  "warnings": [],
  "errors": []
}

request.description: Pass a system prompt via context.system_prompt. This is the standard way to set agent persona / instructions.

response.name: 200 OK — bulleted response
response.body:
{
  "output": "- Multimodal models dominate new releases\n- Agentic systems with tool use are mainstream\n- On-device inference growing fast\n- Open-source models closing the gap with proprietary\n- Inference cost dropping 10x year-over-year",
  "tool_traces": [],
  "usage": { "input_tokens": 32, "output_tokens": 58 },
  "provider": "openai_compatible",
  "model": "gpt-4o",
  "warnings": [],
  "errors": []
}

request.description: agent_id + profile together form the lookup key. Lookup order: 'marketing-bot:default' → 'default' → fallback. Useful for multi-tenant deployments.

response.name: 200 OK
response.body:
{
  "output": "We're thrilled to announce Unified Agents SDK v2.0 — now with native MCP support and per-request context injection!",
  "tool_traces": [],
  "usage": { "input_tokens": 18, "output_tokens": 31 },
  "provider": "openai_compatible",
  "model": "gpt-4o",
  "warnings": [],
  "errors": []
}

item.name: 2 — Provider Profiles
item.description: Select different LLM backends via the 'profile' field. Profiles are defined in AGENT_PROFILES in .env.

request.description: Explicit 'default' profile — resolves to openai_compatible provider with DEFAULT_OPENAI_MODEL from .env.

response.name: 200 OK — OpenAI
response.body:
{
  "output": "Quantum entanglement is when two particles become linked so that measuring one instantly affects the other, no matter how far apart they are.",
  "tool_traces": [],
  "usage": { "input_tokens": 10, "output_tokens": 34 },
  "provider": "openai_compatible",
  "model": "gpt-4o",
  "warnings": [],
  "errors": []
}

request.description:
Requires AGENT_PROFILES to have a 'claude' entry in .env:
  AGENT_PROFILES={"claude":{"provider_name":"anthropic","model":"claude-opus-4-5"}}

response.name: 200 OK — Anthropic
response.body:
{
  "output": "Nodes whisper in time\nConsensus blooms like spring rain\nFault tolerance holds",
  "tool_traces": [],
  "usage": { "input_tokens": 12, "output_tokens": 17 },
  "provider": "anthropic",
  "model": "claude-opus-4-5",
  "warnings": [],
  "errors": []
}

request.description:
Requires AGENT_PROFILES to have a 'gemini' entry:
  AGENT_PROFILES={"gemini":{"provider_name":"gemini","model":"gemini-2.5-flash"}}

response.name: 200 OK — Gemini
response.body:
{
  "output": "1. Medical image analysis\n2. Clinical note summarisation\n3. Drug interaction checking",
  "tool_traces": [],
  "usage": { "input_tokens": 13, "output_tokens": 20 },
  "provider": "gemini",
  "model": "gemini-2.5-flash",
  "warnings": [],
  "errors": []
}

response.name: 200 OK — Groq/Llama
response.body:
{
  "output": "Spanish: Hola mundo\nFrench: Bonjour le monde\nJapanese: ハローワールド",
  "tool_traces": [],
  "usage": { "input_tokens": 16, "output_tokens": 22 },
  "provider": "openai_compatible",
  "model": "llama-3.3-70b-versatile",
  "warnings": [],
  "errors": []
}

response.name: 200 OK — DeepSeek

request.description: Extra generation options are passed through to the provider. temperature, max_tokens, top_p etc. are forwarded as-is.

response.name: 200 OK — with options
response.body:
{
  "output": "A large language model is a neural network trained on vast text corpora to predict and generate natural language.",
  "tool_traces": [],
  "usage": { "input_tokens": 13, "output_tokens": 24 },
  "provider": "openai_compatible",
  "model": "gpt-4o",
  "warnings": [],
  "errors": []
}

response.name: 200 OK
response.body:
{
  "output": "An embedding is a dense vector representation of text or data.",
  "tool_traces": [],
  "usage": {},
  "provider": "openai_responses",
  "model": "gpt-4o",
  "warnings": [],
  "errors": []
}

request.description:
Requires AGENT_PROFILES with provider_name xai and XAI_API_KEY in .env, e.g.:
  {"grok":{"provider_name":"xai","model":"grok-2-latest"}}

response.name: 200 OK
response.body:
{
  "output": "Real-time web-grounded answers.",
  "tool_traces": [],
  "usage": {},
  "provider": "xai",
  "model": "grok-2-latest",
  "warnings": [],
  "errors": []
}

request.description:
Requires MISTRAL_API_KEY and AGENT_PROFILES, e.g.:
  {"mistral":{"provider_name":"mistral","model":"mistral-large-latest"}}

response.name: 200 OK
response.body:
{
  "output": "Guten Morgen",
  "tool_traces": [],
  "usage": {},
  "provider": "mistral",
  "model": "mistral-large-latest",
  "warnings": [],
  "errors": []
}

item.name: 3 — Named Presets (from .env)
item.description: Reference MCP servers and context sources configured in .env. Credentials stay server-side — callers only pass namespace/name strings.

response.name: 200 OK — MCP tool used

request.description: Multiple MCP servers activated simultaneously. Each gets its own session and namespace prefix. Tools: 'search.*' and 'files.*'.

response.name: 200 OK — multi-MCP
200 OK — with context
response.body:
{
  "output": "Dear valued customer, we sincerely apologise for the delay in your shipment. As a global platform, we occasionally experience carrier disruptions, but we're actively working to resolve this. Your order is now prioritised and will arrive within 2 business days. We'd like to offer you a 10% discount on your next order as a gesture of goodwill.",
  "tool_traces": [],
  "usage": { "input_tokens": 92, "output_tokens": 76 },
  "provider": "openai_compatible",
  "model": "gpt-4o",
  "warnings": [],
  "errors": []
}

item.name: 3.4 Profile with built-in presets — no runtime needed

response.name: 200 OK — profile-driven presets

request.description: Combines a profile-level preset ('researcher' profile auto-includes 'search' MCP from .env) with a one-off inline HTTP tool added just for this request.

response.name: 200 OK

item.name: 4 — Inline MCP Servers
item.description: Provide full MCP server connection details inline in the request. Best for ad-hoc or user-supplied MCP servers.

request.description: Full inline MCP server spec. transport defaults to 'streamable_http' (MCP spec >= 2024-11-05). Tools discovered at request time and registered under 'search.*'.

response.name: 200 OK — MCP tools discovered

item.name: 4.2 Single MCP server — SSE legacy transport

request.description: Use transport: 'sse' for older MCP servers (pre-2024-11-05). Longer timeout for large file reads.

response.name: 200 OK — SSE MCP

request.description: Pass arbitrary headers to the MCP server. Use for OAuth tokens, tenant IDs, API keys, etc. These are forwarded on every MCP request during the session.

response.name: 200 OK — GitHub issue created

request.description: Multiple fully-specified MCP servers in a single request. Each runs as an independent session with its own namespace. Sessions are all cleaned up when the request completes.

response.name: 200 OK — multi-session MCP

item.name: 4.5 MCP server with top-level namespace prefix

request.description: runtime.namespace adds a prefix to ALL tools and contexts registered in this request. Result: tools are registered as 'tenant_a.search.*'. Useful for multi-tenant isolation.

response.name: 200 OK — namespaced tools

item.name: 5 — Inline HTTP Tools
item.description: Define arbitrary HTTP-callable tools inline in the request body. No MCP server needed — any REST endpoint can be a tool.

item.name: 5.1 Single HTTP tool — POST JSON body

request.description: argument_mode: 'json' sends the model's tool arguments as a JSON body. The response (JSON or text) is returned to the model.

response.name: 200 OK — tool called

item.name: 5.2 HTTP tool — GET with query params

request.description: argument_mode: 'query' sends tool arguments as URL query parameters (?city=London&units=metric). Ideal for REST GET APIs.

response.name: 200 OK — weather fetched

request.description: Two tools with different methods and argument modes. The agent can call them in sequence (multi-hop). Each tool can have its own timeout.

response.name: 200 OK — multi-tool

request.description: Tool-level headers are forwarded with every call to that tool's URL. Use for bearer tokens, API keys, or any fixed headers the tool service requires.

response.name: 200 OK

item.name: 5.5 Isolated — no global tools, inline only

request.description: use_global_tools: false means the agent starts with a blank tool registry. Only the tools defined in this request's runtime.tools are available. Good for sandboxed or restricted execution.

response.name: 200 OK — isolated toolset

item.name: 6 — Inline Contexts
item.description: Inject background context (RAG results, company info, user profile, etc.) into the agent's system prompt. Static text, HTTP fetch, ContextForge, or dynamic markdown (md_hierarchy / md_files / md_glob) — same fields as README.

request.description: Static text is injected directly into the system prompt. No external calls. Best for small, known strings (company name, persona, rules).

response.name: 200 OK — context injected
response.body:
{
  "output": "Our refund policy offers a full refund within 30 days, no questions asked. After 30 days we can offer store credit. Note that software licences and downloadable content are non-refundable.",
  "tool_traces": [],
  "usage": { "input_tokens": 72, "output_tokens": 44 },
  "provider": "openai_compatible",
  "model": "gpt-4o",
  "warnings": [],
  "errors": []
}

request.description: Curly-brace placeholders in context text are substituted from the 'context' dict. {username} → 'Alice', {plan} → 'Pro'. Unknown placeholders are left as-is (safe formatting).

response.name: 200 OK — personalised
response.body:
{
  "output": "Welcome back, Alice! As a Pro member you have access to unlimited exports, priority support, and our advanced analytics dashboard. Let us know if you need anything!",
  "tool_traces": [],
  "usage": { "input_tokens": 56, "output_tokens": 38 },
  "provider": "openai_compatible",
  "model": "gpt-4o",
  "warnings": [],
  "errors": []
}

request.description: Fetches relevant docs from a RAG endpoint before calling the LLM. The gateway POSTs the payload_template (with {input} substituted) to the URL, then injects the response text into the system prompt. required: true means a fetch failure raises an error rather than silently continuing.

response.name: 200 OK — RAG context injected
response.body:
{
  "output": "To configure SSO with Okta: 1) Go to Settings > Security > SSO. 2) Copy the SAML metadata URL from your Okta app. 3) Paste it into the 'IdP Metadata URL' field. 4) Map email attribute to user.email. 5) Save and test with a non-admin account first.",
  "tool_traces": [],
  "usage": { "input_tokens": 312, "output_tokens": 78 },
  "provider": "openai_compatible",
  "model": "gpt-4o",
  "warnings": [],
  "errors": []
}

item.name: 6.4 HTTP context — GET with query params

request.description: method: GET sends payload_template as query string (?id=42). The user_id comes from context dict via {user_id} substitution.

response.name: 200 OK — profile fetched
response.body:
{
  "output": "Yes, user 42 (Sarah Connor) is on the Business plan, active since Jan 2024.",
  "tool_traces": [],
  "usage": { "input_tokens": 88, "output_tokens": 22 },
  "provider": "openai_compatible",
  "model": "gpt-4o",
  "warnings": [],
  "errors": []
}

request.description: Both contexts are injected in order. All [name] sections appear under 'Additional context:' in the system prompt. The model sees: coding_style rules + relevant code snippets from RAG.

response.name: 200 OK — multi-context
response.body:
{
  "output": "The error means a variable is None when you try to call .split() on it. Add a guard clause:\n\ndef process(value: str | None) -> list:\n    if value is None:\n        return []\n    return value.split()",
  "tool_traces": [],
  "usage": { "input_tokens": 198, "output_tokens": 56 },
  "provider": "openai_compatible",
  "model": "gpt-4o",
  "warnings": [],
  "errors": []
}

item.name: 6.6 Isolated — no global contexts, inline only

request.description: use_global_contexts: false starts from an empty context registry. Only the contexts defined here are injected. Prevents any server-side global context from leaking into this call.

response.name: 200 OK — isolated context
response.body:
{
  "output": "TCP/IP is basically the rulebook that computers follow when they talk to each other over the internet — it decides how to package, address, send, and receive information.",
  "tool_traces": [],
  "usage": { "input_tokens": 78, "output_tokens": 38 },
  "provider": "openai_compatible",
  "model": "gpt-4o",
  "warnings": [],
  "errors": []
}

request.description: ContextForge inline — passes the query to your ContextForge server and injects the result. Alternatively configure CONTEXTFORGE_URL + CONTEXTFORGE_API_KEY in .env to use globally.

response.name: 200 OK — ContextForge injected
response.body:
{
  "output": "Q2 roadmap highlights: Launch multi-region support (April), Ship mobile app v2 (May), GA of the API gateway (June). Three major features, 12 bug-fix releases planned.",
  "tool_traces": [],
  "usage": { "input_tokens": 280, "output_tokens": 48 },
  "provider": "openai_compatible",
  "model": "gpt-4o",
  "warnings": [],
  "errors": []
}

request.description: Per-request hierarchical markdown collection (same utility as global harness). Walks cwd and ancestors for each filename. See README **Request Schema** → runtime.contexts and api/http.py DynamicContext.

response.name: 200 OK
response.body:
{
  "output": "Project rules: ...",
  "tool_traces": [],
  "usage": {},
  "provider": "openai_compatible",
  "model": "gpt-4o",
  "warnings": [],
  "errors": []
}

request.description: Reads explicit file paths (absolute or relative to cwd). Missing files are skipped with a warning.

response.name: 200 OK
response.body:
{
  "output": "This repository is the Unified Agents SDK.",
  "tool_traces": [],
  "usage": {},
  "provider": "openai_compatible",
  "model": "gpt-4o",
  "warnings": [],
  "errors": []
}

request.description: Collects all files matching glob_pattern under each glob_dir. Empty dirs produce no text (may warn).

response.name: 200 OK
response.body:
{
  "output": "Docs cover API, MCP, and deployment.",
  "tool_traces": [],
  "usage": {},
  "provider": "openai_compatible",
  "model": "gpt-4o",
  "warnings": [],
  "errors": []
}

item.name: 7 — Kitchen Sink (Combined)
item.description: Full combinations showing how every feature works together in a single request.

item.name: 7.1 Profile + named presets + inline tool + inline context

request.description:
Every feature at once:
- Profile 'researcher' (from .env) auto-includes its presets
- Named MCP namespace 'search' (from .env)
- Named context 'company_info' (from .env)
- One-off inline HTTP tool 'weather'
- One-off inline static context 'user_prefs' with template
- Top-level namespace 'briefing' prefixes all inline registrations
- System prompt via context.system_prompt
- temperature option forwarded to provider

response.name: 200 OK — full combined

item.name: 7.2 Fully isolated — no globals, all inline

request.description: Completely sandboxed execution: no global tools, no global contexts. The agent only has the two tools and one context defined in this request. Nothing from .env registries leaks in.

response.name: 200 OK — sandboxed

item.name: 7.3 Inline MCP + inline tool + RAG context

request.description: Real-world DevOps scenario: RAG fetches deployment docs → agent identifies missing step → uses JIRA MCP to create a ticket → uses inline HTTP tool to add a doc reference comment.

response.name: 200 OK — DevOps scenario

item.name: 8 — Streaming (SSE)
item.description: POST /agent-query/stream returns Server-Sent Events. Same request body as /agent-query. Events: 'chunk' (text delta), 'tool_call', 'done', 'warning', 'error'.

response.name: 200 OK — SSE stream

item.name: 8.2 Stream with system prompt and profile

request.description: Stream with Anthropic Claude profile. Same profile/context support as sync endpoint.

response.name: 200 OK — Claude streaming

request.description: Context injection works identically in the streaming endpoint. The context is fetched/resolved before streaming begins.

response.name: 200 OK — streaming with context

request.description: MCP session is opened before streaming begins and torn down when the client disconnects or the stream ends. Tool calls happen synchronously mid-stream and are surfaced as 'tool_call' events.

response.name: 200 OK — MCP + stream

request.description: When a named MCP namespace is not found in settings, the stream starts with a 'warning' event, then continues normally without the missing tools.

response.name: 200 OK — warning then content

item.name: 9 — Error Cases
item.description: Expected error responses and non-fatal warning scenarios.

request.description: When ALLOW_DYNAMIC_RUNTIME_REGISTRATION=false in .env, any request that tries to add inline tools, MCP servers, or contexts gets a 403. Set to 'true' (the default) to allow it.

response.name: 403 Forbidden

item.name: 9.2 Unknown MCP namespace — warning, not error

request.description: A namespace not found in MCP_SERVERS settings is skipped with a warning. The request succeeds — warnings array in the response tells you what was skipped.

response.name: 200 OK with warning
response.body:
{
  "output": "Hello! How can I help you today?",
  "tool_traces": [],
  "usage": { "input_tokens": 5, "output_tokens": 9 },
  "provider": "openai_compatible",
  "model": "gpt-4o",
  "warnings": ["MCP namespace 'does_not_exist' not found in MCP_SERVERS settings — skipped"],
  "errors": []
}

item.name: 9.3 Unknown context name — warning, not error

request.description: Same pattern as unknown MCP namespace — missing context names are skipped with a warning, not an error. Unless the context is marked required: true.

response.name: 200 OK with warning
response.body:
{
  "output": "I can help with questions, analysis, coding, writing, and much more!",
  "tool_traces": [],
  "usage": { "input_tokens": 7, "output_tokens": 16 },
  "provider": "openai_compatible",
  "model": "gpt-4o",
  "warnings": ["Context name 'undefined_context' not found in NAMED_CONTEXTS settings — skipped"],
  "errors": []
}

item.name: 9.4 MCP server connection failure — warning, not error

request.description: If an inline MCP server is unreachable, the connection error is collected as a warning. The request proceeds without those tools. The agent's response may reflect that the tool was unavailable.

response.name: 200 OK — MCP failure degraded gracefully
response.body:
{
  "output": "I wasn't able to perform a search as the search service appears to be unavailable. Please try again later.",
  "tool_traces": [],
  "usage": { "input_tokens": 18, "output_tokens": 22 },
  "provider": "openai_compatible",
  "model": "gpt-4o",
  "warnings": ["Inline MCP server at 'http://offline-mcp.internal/mcp' (namespace 'broken') failed to connect: ConnectionRefusedError"],
  "errors": []
}

item.name: 9.5 HTTP context with no URL — skipped with warning

request.description: A context with mode: 'http' but no url is silently skipped with a warning. Useful to catch misconfigured payloads during development.

response.name: 200 OK — context skipped
response.body:
{
  "output": "I don't have specific information about your company loaded right now.",
  "tool_traces": [],
  "usage": { "input_tokens": 9, "output_tokens": 14 },
  "provider": "openai_compatible",
  "model": "gpt-4o",
  "warnings": ["Context 'company_info' skipped: mode=http but no url provided"],
  "errors": []
}

item.name: 9.6 Provider error (bad API key) — 500 with errors array

request.description: When the upstream provider returns an auth or API error, the gateway catches it as a GatewayError and returns it in the errors array with an appropriate HTTP status code.

response.name: 401 — provider auth error
response.body:
{
  "output": "",
  "tool_traces": [],
  "usage": {},
  "provider": "openai_compatible",
  "model": null,
  "warnings": [],
  "errors": ["[openai_compatible] Authentication failed: Incorrect API key provided"]
}

item.name: 10 — Provider credentials (BYOK)
item.description:
Optional `provider_credentials` on the request body overrides the resolved LLM api_key / model / base_url for that call only.

Requires server env: ALLOW_PER_REQUEST_PROVIDER_CREDENTIALS=true (default false).

Postman variable `{{openai_key}}` is NOT magic: it only substitutes into the JSON so you do not hard-code secrets. The gateway still receives that value inside `provider_credentials.api_key` (BYOK path). If you omit `provider_credentials` entirely, the gateway uses OPENAI_API_KEY (or profile keys) from its own .env — no Postman variable involved.

Security: keys in JSON bodies are logged by many proxies — use only behind your own auth, or prefer .env + AGENT_PROFILES.

request.description:
Full override for OpenAI-compatible routing. Omit base_url to use the profile/env default base URL.

Set ALLOW_PER_REQUEST_PROVIDER_CREDENTIALS=true on the gateway before sending.

response.name: 200 OK — used caller key
response.body:
{
  "output": "Hello — glad to meet you!",
  "tool_traces": [],
  "usage": { "input_tokens": 12, "output_tokens": 8 },
  "provider": "openai_compatible",
  "model": "gpt-4o-mini",
  "warnings": [],
  "errors": []
}

request.description: Only api_key is sent; model and base_url stay whatever the profile + env resolved (e.g. gpt-4o from DEFAULT_OPENAI_MODEL).

response.name: 200 OK
response.body:
{
  "output": "2 + 2 equals 4.",
  "tool_traces": [],
  "usage": { "input_tokens": 8, "output_tokens": 6 },
  "provider": "openai_compatible",
  "model": "gpt-4o",
  "warnings": [],
  "errors": []
}

request.description: profile still selects built-in openai_compatible adapter; credentials override point traffic at Groq (or any OpenAI-compatible endpoint) for this request only.

response.name: 200 OK — Groq
response.body:
{
  "output": "Lower latency improves interactive apps and reduces user wait time.",
  "tool_traces": [],
  "usage": { "input_tokens": 14, "output_tokens": 12 },
  "provider": "openai_compatible",
  "model": "llama-3.3-70b-versatile",
  "warnings": [],
  "errors": []
}

request.description: For anthropic profile, api_key and model override env/profile. base_url in provider_credentials is ignored by the Anthropic SDK adapter.

response.name: 200 OK
response.body:
{
  "output": "pong",
  "tool_traces": [],
  "usage": { "input_tokens": 10, "output_tokens": 2 },
  "provider": "anthropic",
  "model": "claude-sonnet-4-20250514",
  "warnings": [],
  "errors": []
}

request.description: Same provider_credentials apply to /agent-query/stream. Response is text/event-stream.

response.name: 200 OK — SSE

request.description:
Default gateway: ALLOW_PER_REQUEST_PROVIDER_CREDENTIALS=false. Any non-empty credential field triggers 403.

Enable in .env: ALLOW_PER_REQUEST_PROVIDER_CREDENTIALS=true

response.name: 403 Forbidden

request.description: An empty object (or all fields null/omitted) does not count as BYOK — allowed even when ALLOW_PER_REQUEST_PROVIDER_CREDENTIALS=false.

response.name: 200 OK
response.body:
{
  "output": "Hi! How can I help?",
  "tool_traces": [],
  "usage": { "input_tokens": 4, "output_tokens": 7 },
  "provider": "openai_compatible",
  "model": "gpt-4o",
  "warnings": [],
  "errors": []
}

item.name: 11 — MCP README scenarios (bridges & paths)
item.description: Mirrors README **MCP — Model Context Protocol**: (11.1) gateway Path 1 with the `github_mcp` named preset from COPILOT_MCP_BRIDGE; (11.2) provider Path 2 with options.mcp_servers on OpenAI Responses; (11.3) Claude Agent agent-managed MCP. Bridges only register presets — you still pass mcp_namespaces unless using global Codex tools.

item.name: 11.1 Gateway Path 1 — named github_mcp (Copilot MCP bridge)

request.description:
Gateway connects to GitHub's remote MCP and executes tools locally (Path 1).

Requires on the server:
  COPILOT_MCP_BRIDGE=true
  COPILOT_GITHUB_TOKEN=ghp_...   (or GH_TOKEN / GITHUB_TOKEN)

Optional: COPILOT_MCP_TOOLSETS as JSON list in .env.

The bridge merges preset key `github_mcp` into MCP_SERVERS at bootstrap — same as README **MCP bridges** table.

response.name: 200 OK
response.body:
{
  "output": "I can access GitHub repos, issues, and PRs exposed by the MCP server.",
  "tool_traces": [],

request.description:
OpenAI's backend connects to the MCP server directly; the gateway AgentLoop does not execute those tool calls (Path 2).

Requires profile with provider_name openai_responses (e.g. openai-r). Set {{mcp_server_url}} to a real streamable HTTP MCP endpoint.

See README **Provider Path 2** and providers/openai_responses.py.

response.name: 200 OK
response.body:
{
  "output": "MCP is a protocol for tool and context exchange between hosts and servers.",
  "tool_traces": [],
  "usage": {},
  "provider": "openai_responses",
  "model": "gpt-4o",
  "warnings": [],
  "errors": []
}

item.name: 11.3 Claude Agent — agent-managed mcp_servers in options

request.description:
Claude Agent SDK runs its own subprocess and MCP lifecycle; runtime.mcp_namespaces is NOT used for this path.

Requires: pip install 'unified-agents-sdk[claude-agent]' and AGENT_PROFILES entry claude_agent with provider_name claude_agent.

Replace BRAVE_API_KEY or swap mcp_servers for your real stdio MCP. README **Claude Agent SDK** section.

response.name: 200 OK
response.body:
{
  "output": "I can call tools exposed by the MCP server.",
  "tool_traces": [],
  "usage": {},
  "provider": "claude_agent",
  "model": "claude-opus-4-5",
  "warnings": [],
  "errors": []
}

item.name: 11.4 Codex MCP tools on another provider (inverted bridge)

request.description:
When CODEX_MCP_ENABLED=true at gateway bootstrap, tools from `codex mcp-server` are registered globally under namespace `codex`.

Use profile **claude** (or any tool-capable provider) with default use_global_tools: true — NOT profile codex. The codex provider ignores gateway ToolRegistry.

README **Codex CLI** inverted bridge.

response.name: 200 OK
response.body:
{
  "output": "If codex.* tools are registered globally, I can invoke them from this profile.",
  "tool_traces": [],
  "usage": {},

  "model": "claude-opus-4-5",

}
